name: Toga / BeeWare (Native Python Mobile+Desktop)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD); BeeWare project / Russell Keith-Magee

performance.notes: Native widgets on each platform; near-native performance

agent_readiness.notes: Native widget toolkit for Python. Deploy Python apps to macOS, Windows, Linux, iOS, Android, Web, tvOS. App, Window, Box, Button, Label, WebView, ScrollContainer. briefcase build/run for multi-platform deployment. Uses native platform widgets (UIKit on iOS, Android Views, Win32 on Windows). Write once, deploy everywhere in Python. Supported by Meta. Limited widget set compared to Flutter/React Native. Still maturing — not all widgets on all platforms. Kivy is alternative for mobile.

gotchas: Limited widget set — not all widgets available on all platforms
Python startup time on mobile can be slow
Smaller community than React Native or Flutter
best_when: Python teams wanting mobile/desktop apps without JavaScript
Simple utility apps targeting multiple platforms from Python
Organizations wanting to leverage existing Python skills for mobile
Academic/research tools needing desktop + mobile deployment
avoid_when: Production consumer mobile apps (React Native/Flutter more mature)
summary: Toga (BeeWare) lets Python developers build native apps for macOS, Windows, Linux, iOS, Android, and Web using native platform widgets. briefcase build/run handles packaging. Write once, deploy everywhere. Limited widget set but growing. Best for Python teams wanting mobile deployment without learning JavaScript frameworks. React Native/Flutter are more mature for production consumer apps.
